name: Hide from Linarejos — circular on the Mill Route
name_original: Mahide desde Linarejos — circular por la Ruta los Molinos
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Manzanal De Arriba traverse the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de la Culebra in Zamora, Spain. The region is characterized by extensive oak and chestnut forests, undulating hills, and deep valleys carved by rivers and streams. This diverse landscape offers varied terrain suitable for mountain biking, from forested paths to more open sections. Best mountain bike trails around Manzanal De Arriba The most popular mountain bike trail is La Carballeda BTT Trail, a 27.7 miles (44.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 35 minutes to complete. This route offers a journey through the region's characteristic woodlands and hills. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Curucuta Route, a difficult 34.4 miles (55.3 km) path. This trail features significant elevation changes, exploring the higher points and valleys of the Sierra de la Culebra. Local mountain bikers also love the Alto de San Juan and Palacios de Sanabria Loop, a 31.6 miles (50.9 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes and offering expansive views, often completed in about 4 hours 50 minutes. Mountain biking around Manzanal De Arriba is defined by mountainous terrain, oak and chestnut forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and difficult routes. The routes in Manzanal De Arriba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 150 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Manzanal De Arriba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Manzanal De Arriba?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Manzanal De Arriba, offering a variety of experiences. These include 27 moderate routes and 6 more challenging options, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the mountain bike trails in Manzanal De Arriba?
A: The terrain around Manzanal De Arriba is predominantly mountainous, characterized by undulating hills and extensive &lt;strong&gt;oak and chestnut forests&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll find paths winding through these woodlands, alongside river valleys, and across more open sections, providing a diverse and scenic riding experience within the Sierra de la Culebra.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018857698/curucuta-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Curucuta Route&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation changes over its 34.4 miles (55.3 km). Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222869418/alto-de-san-juan-and-palacios-de-sanabria-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de San Juan and Palacios de Sanabria Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 31.6 miles (50.9 km) through mountainous landscapes with over 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Many routes in Manzanal De Arriba are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222869418/alto-de-san-juan-and-palacios-de-sanabria-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de San Juan and Palacios de Sanabria Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular trail. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222097394/linarejos-and-rihonor-de-castilla-from-robledo-circular-route-through-the-sierra-de-la-culebra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Linarejos and Rihonor de Castilla from Robledo — circular route through the Sierra de la Culebra&lt;/a&gt;, offering a moderate ride through varied landscapes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking?
A: While mountain biking, you can explore the rich natural beauty of the Sierra de la Culebra. Keep an eye out for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7372143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centennial Pyrenean Oak (Carvalho-Negral)&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7370662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Rolling Hills in Montesinho Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features charming river areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5814010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Weir and Boardwalk of the Rio Onor&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. You might encounter traditional &lt;strong&gt;stone houses&lt;/strong&gt; in villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1679368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio de Onor Village&lt;/a&gt;. The region also has ancient roots, with prehistoric schematic art found in the Covacha del Portillón cave and remnants of the Roman road Via XVII. You can also visit historical religious sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3866268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Incarnation&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5458087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Remedies, Otero de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Manzanal De Arriba?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Manzanal De Arriba is typically during the &lt;strong&gt;summer months&lt;/strong&gt;, when mountain temperatures are cooler and the landscapes are lush and green. &lt;strong&gt;Autumn&lt;/strong&gt; also offers a spectacular experience with vibrant foliage and seasonal produce, making it a beautiful time to explore the trails.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: While the Sierra de la Culebra is a natural habitat for wildlife, including wolves, many trails in the region are suitable for dogs. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to check local regulations for specific routes to ensure a safe and enjoyable experience for everyone.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Manzanal De Arriba?
A: Manzanal De Arriba offers a range of trails, including moderate options that can be suitable for families with some biking experience. While specific 'easy' trails are not highlighted, the varied terrain allows for choosing less strenuous sections. The extensive network of forest paths and access roads can provide gentler rides, especially if you focus on routes with less elevation gain.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Manzanal De Arriba?
A: The mountain biking experience in Manzanal De Arriba is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Riders frequently praise the region's diverse terrain, the beauty of the &lt;strong&gt;oak and chestnut forests&lt;/strong&gt;, and the tranquility of the Sierra de la Culebra, which offers a sense of immersion in nature with minimal human presence.

Q: Are there any opportunities for wild swimming or water-based activities near the trails?
A: Yes, the region offers opportunities for water-based recreation. Manzanal de Arriba has a tranquil 'beach' area near the Los Molinos campsite, suggesting possibilities for activities like canoeing or pedal boating on a local body of water. You might also find refreshing spots along the rivers and streams that crisscross the area, such as the Cabrón stream and the Manzanas river.

Q: Do I need any special permits for mountain biking in the Sierra de la Culebra?
A: Generally, mountain biking on established trails in the Sierra de la Culebra does not require special permits for individual riders. However, it's always advisable to respect local signage, stay on marked paths, and be aware of any specific regulations, especially if you are part of a larger group or event. The area is known for its wildlife, so responsible riding is encouraged.
tips: Peña Mira, at 1,241 meters above sea level, is the highest point in the Sierra de la Culebra in Zamora. It offers us magnificent views, to the northwest, of the town of Linarejos, and further north after the Lago de Sanabria area, three other towns in this beautiful Zamoran environment.
Mahide is a small hamlet in the Aliste region, in the province of Zamora, surrounded by a natural environment of great value such as the Sierra de la Culebra.
Beautiful town to come with the children to walk and enjoy the Arroyo Carbón.
Gravel path through the Carbón stream, until reaching the rock formation called Antigua Losera de Figueruela. The path runs along the slopes of Peña Mira.
Linarejos is a picturesque town near the border with Portugal. Among its houses many traditional stone houses are preserved, some modernized. Here you can find rural houses and even a bar.

waypoints.name: Carbón Stream and Antigua Losera de Figueruela
